page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.re6.52xlarge is a memory-optimized virtual machine instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with 208 dedicated physical cores and 208 vCPUs, utilizing Intel Xeon 8269CL processors. The instance operates with a 1.0 thread-per-core configuration, meaning hyperthreading is disabled. It is equipped with 3072.0 GB of system memory, yielding approximately 14.77 GB of RAM per core.

This instance does not include local storage, requiring external block storage volumes for persistent data. It also lacks GPU acceleration, making it unsuitable for hardware-accelerated graphics or deep learning training. For network connectivity, the instance provides a baseline network bandwidth of 32 Gbps.

Given its massive memory footprint and high physical core count, the ecs.re6.52xlarge is positioned in a premium hardware class with high resource density. It is qualitatively cost-efficient for workloads that require massive RAM capacity without the need for local NVMe storage or GPU resources. The hardware profile is designed for large-scale in-memory databases, enterprise resource planning systems, and heavy memory-intensive data processing applications.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.re6.52xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 208 dedicated physical cores and 208 vCPUs on an Intel Xeon 8269CL x86_64 architecture. Running on the KVM hypervisor, this instance provides 3072.0 GB of system memory, which equates to 14.77 GB of RAM per core, with hyperthreading disabled. It lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, relying instead on external block storage and offering a baseline network bandwidth of 32 Gbps. The high resource density of this instance makes it qualitatively cost-effective for large-scale in-memory databases, enterprise ERP systems, and memory-intensive data analytics workloads that do not require local NVMe drives or specialized GPU hardware.

tagline: A massive memory-optimized instance featuring dedicated Intel Xeon cores and three terabytes of RAM for large-scale in-memory enterprise workloads.
